skill.name: triage
skill.description: Triage issues through a state machine driven by triage roles. Use when user wants to create an issue, triage issues, review incoming bugs or feature requests, prepare issues for an AFK agent, or manage issue workflow.
skill.summary: State machine-driven issue triage skill for managing bug reports and feature requests through a structured workflow.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation-only skill that guides issue triage workflows. Static analysis flagged 115 potential issues, but all are false positives: Markdown code fences were misidentified as Ruby shell execution, and documentation examples mentioning 'algorithm' were misidentified as weak cryptography. The skill uses standard GitHub API tools to manage issue states - legitimate functionality with no malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Static Analysis False Positives - Backtick Pattern Misidentification
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 84 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' but these are actually Markdown code fences (``` ```) used in documentation files to display example code. No shell execution occurs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Markdown code fences are clearly visible as triple-backtick blocks in documentation files with language annotations - standard documentation practice

medium_findings.title: Static Analysis False Positives - Algorithm Term Misidentification
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 30 instances of 'weak cryptographic algorithm' but these are examples using the word 'algorithm' in its general sense (approach/method). SKILL.md:3 uses 'algorithm' to mean a methodical approach to triage, not cryptography.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged context shows 'algorithm' used in documentation discussing approaches and methods, not cryptographic operations

content.user_title: Triage GitHub Issues with a State Machine
content.value_statement: Managing incoming issues without a structured workflow leads to bottlenecks and missed priorities. This skill provides a state machine-driven triage system that categorizes issues as bugs or enhancements and routes them through defined states from needs-triage to ready-for-agent or wontfix.
actual_capabilities: Query GitHub issues and present unlabeled, needs-triage, and needs-info issues requiring re-evaluation
Apply category labels (bug/enhancement) and state labels (needs-triage, needs-info, ready-for-agent, ready-for-human, wontfix) to issues
Generate structured agent briefs with behavioral specifications for issues ready for AI agents
Write durable agent briefs that avoid file paths and line numbers for longevity
Maintain an out-of-scope knowledge base for rejected feature requests to prevent re-litigation
Check existing triage notes and resume sessions without re-asking resolved questions
limitations: Requires GitHub API access and appropriate repository permissions
Does not automatically close issues or take action without maintainer direction
Cannot grill reporters without the grill-with-docs skill being available
Cannot verify bug reproduction without a working development environment

use_cases.title: Weekly Issue Triage
use_cases.description: Review incoming issues once a week, categorize them as bugs or enhancements, and route them to appropriate states. Present a prioritized list to the maintainer for direction.
use_cases.target_user: Project maintainers with regular incoming issue volume

use_cases.title: Preparing Work for AFK Agents
use_cases.description: When an issue is fully specified and ready for autonomous work, generate a durable agent brief that will remain useful even as the codebase evolves.
use_cases.target_user: Team leads delegating work to AI agents

use_cases.title: Deduplicating Feature Requests
use_cases.description: When a new feature request arrives, check the out-of-scope knowledge base and surface prior rejections that match the concept.

prompt_templates.title: Show Issues Needing Attention
prompt_templates.prompt: /triage - Show me anything that needs my attention
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: Quick overview of pending triage items

prompt_templates.title: Triage a Specific Issue
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: Focus on a single issue for evaluation

prompt_templates.title: Move Issue to Ready-for-Agent
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: Apply a specific state transition

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Issue with Agent Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: /triage - #67 is ready, write an agent brief for it
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: Generate complete agent brief for autonomous work

output_examples.input: /triage - Show me anything that needs my attention
output_examples.output:
3 issues need your attention:

**Unlabeled (3)**
- #142: App crashes on startup with large datasets
- #143: Export to PDF not working
- #144: Add dark mode support

**Needs-triage (1)**
- #139: Memory leak in worker process (evaluation in progress)

**Needs-info with recent activity (1)**
- #87: @user replied to your questions about the API

output_examples.input: /triage - #67 is ready, write an agent brief for it
output_examples.output:
## Agent Brief

**Category:** enhancement
**Summary:** Add keyboard shortcut support for common actions

**Current behavior:** Users must navigate menus to perform frequent actions like save, copy, and paste.

**Desired behavior:** Keyboard shortcuts for save (Ctrl+S), copy (Ctrl+C), paste (Ctrl+V), and undo (Ctrl+Z) should work globally.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Ctrl+S saves the current document
- [ ] Ctrl+C copies selected content
- [ ] Ctrl+V pastes clipboard content
- [ ] Ctrl+Z undoes the last action
- [ ] Shortcuts work even when focus is on nested widgets

best_practices: Start with the full triage view to get context before drilling into specific issues
Always verify bug reproduction before marking an issue as ready-for-agent
Write agent briefs that describe interfaces and behaviors, not file paths or implementation details
anti_patterns: Don't reference file paths or line numbers in agent briefs - they go stale
Don't skip the needs-info step when reporters provide vague bug reports
Don't mark enhancement requests as wontfix without documenting the reason in .out-of-scope/

faq.question: What is the difference between ready-for-agent and ready-for-human?
faq.answer: Ready-for-agent issues have complete specifications that an AI can work on autonomously. Ready-for-human issues need human judgment, design decisions, external access, or manual testing that AI cannot perform.

faq.question: How do I resume a triage session after a reporter replies?
faq.answer: Call /triage on the issue. The skill will detect existing triage notes, check if the reporter answered your questions, and present an updated picture before continuing.

faq.question: What makes an agent brief durable?
faq.answer: Durable agent briefs describe interfaces, types, and behavioral contracts rather than file paths or line numbers. They work even as the codebase is refactored and files are renamed.

faq.question: When should I use the out-of-scope knowledge base?
faq.answer: Use it when rejecting feature requests that might recur. Documenting the decision and reasoning prevents re-litigating the same discussion with future contributors.

faq.question: What if the issue has conflicting state roles?
faq.answer: Flag the conflict and ask the maintainer before taking any action. The skill will not proceed with triage until the conflict is resolved.

faq.question: Can I override the triage workflow?
faq.answer: Yes. A maintainer can directly request a specific state transition (e.g., 'move #42 to ready-for-agent'). The skill trusts the maintainer and applies the change directly.
